title: CombineFeatureInput Object
description: This class defines the methods and properties that pertain to the definition of a combine feature.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

properties.name: isKeepToolBodies
properties.description: Gets and sets a boolean value for whether or not the tool bodies are retrained after the combine results. The default value is false.

properties.name: isNewComponent
properties.description: Gets and sets a boolean value for whether or not a new component will be created with the results. The default value is false. In Base feature environment NewComponent does not work.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

properties.name: operation
properties.description: Gets and sets the type of operation performed by the combine. The valid values are JoinFeatureOperation, CutFeatureOperation and IntersectFeatureOperation. The default value is JoinFeatureOperation.

properties.name: targetBaseFeature
properties.description: When creating a feature that is owned by a base feature, set this property to the base feature you want to associate the new feature with. By default, this is null, meaning it will not be associated with a base feature.   Because of a current limitation, if you want to create a feature associated with a base feature, you must set this property AND call the startEdit method of the base feature, create the feature, and then call the finishEdit method of the base feature. The base feature must be in an "edit" state to be able to add any additional items to it.

classType.title: CombineFeatureInput.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

isKeepToolBodies.title: CombineFeatureInput.isKeepToolBodies Property
isKeepToolBodies.description: Gets and sets a boolean value for whether or not the tool bodies are retrained after the combine results. The default value is false.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = combineFeatureInput_var.isKeepToolBodies

# Set the value of the property.
combineFeatureInput_var.isKeepToolBodies = propertyValue

isNewComponent.title: CombineFeatureInput.isNewComponent Property
isNewComponent.description: Gets and sets a boolean value for whether or not a new component will be created with the results. The default value is false. In Base feature environment NewComponent does not work.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = combineFeatureInput_var.isNewComponent

# Set the value of the property.
combineFeatureInput_var.isNewComponent = propertyValue

related_samples.name: combineFeatures.add
related_samples.description: Demonstrates the combineFeatures.add method. To use this sample, have a design open that contains at least two bodies. When you run the sample, you will be prompted to select the bodies and they will joined.

targetBaseFeature.title: CombineFeatureInput.targetBaseFeature Property
targetBaseFeature.description: When creating a feature that is owned by a base feature, set this property to the base feature you want to associate the new feature with. By default, this is null, meaning it will not be associated with a base feature. Because of a current limitation, if you want to create a feature associated with a base feature, you must set this property AND call the startEdit method of the base feature, create the feature, and then call the finishEdit method of the base feature. The base feature must be in an "edit" state to be able to add any additional items to it.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = combineFeatureInput_var.targetBaseFeature

# Set the value of the property.

# Get the value of the property.
